# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmp="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp"
tmp_out="/tmp/myapp-$$.out"

cleanup() {
  rm -f -- "$tmp" "$tmp_out"
}
trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM HUP

: >"$tmp"
: >"$tmp_out"

# Write incoming data to temp file
cat >"$tmp"

# Process data (example: trim trailing whitespace, drop empty lines, sort unique)
sed -e 's/[[:space:]]\+$//' "$tmp" \
  | awk 'NF' \
  | sort -u >"$tmp_out"

# Reuse the same temp file path again (example: add line numbers into the same temp file)
nl -ba "$tmp_out" >"$tmp"

# Read results
cat "$tmp"
